items.title: Dozen more possible human burial sites found at Rottnest Island church

children.content: Authorities believe there may be a dozen more unmarked Aboriginal burial sites near where human remains were found on Western Australia's tourist haven Rottnest Island earlier this month.

gallery.altText: A look over rock groynes at boats in a bay at Rottnest with a lighthouse in the background.

thumbnail.altText: A look over rock groynes at boats in a bay at Rottnest with a lighthouse in the background.

items.title: NDIS cuts a 'slap in the face, punch in the guts' for those needing care in regional Australia

image.altText: A young boy and girl both wearing glasses

children.content: People living with disability in regional areas already face extra hurdles in accessing support and families fear changes to the NDIS could have irreversible consequences.

items.title: From the WA bush to the jungle of Vietnam

children.content: Yamatji man Graham Taylor was just 19 when he shipped off to Vietnam. More than 50 years on, the brotherhood forged in the jungle of Vietnam remains solid.

gallery.altText: An Indigenous man in army uniform leans over a balcony smiling

items.title: Three children attacked by dingoes in national park

children.content: Three children have been attacked by dingoes in Karijini National Park since Saturday, prompting a warning from authorities.

items.title: Alleged threat to ‘blow up’ Perth Airport terminal sparks chaos, lands man in court

children.content: A man who allegedly threatened to "blow up" Perth Airport's main international terminal, forcing its evacuation and causing traveller chaos, has appeared in court.

image.altText: Keiron Beau James Hedges smiles.

items.title: Perth figure skating teen makes history for Australia

children.content: At just 15, Hana Bath became the first Australian female to land a triple axel in competition. The rising star who began her journey on a local rink in Cockburn now has her eye on the 2030 Winter Olympics.

items.title: Fight over legal costs from Gina Rinehart judgement begins

items.title: Husband of former WA politician faces court on 27 child sex abuse charges

children.content: Former Nationals and Independent MLC Louise Kingston watched on as her husband Garry faced fresh child sexual abuse charges in a WA court.

image.altText: A man and a woman cover their faces with pieces of paper as they walk past a brick building.

items.title: 'We're not ready to leave': Inside the messy dynamic of a no-grounds eviction

children.content: Evicting a tenant without cause offers landlords a simple way out, but the issue can be much more complex than it appears. One such example of that complexity is Eleanor Kahlo's case of her no-grounds eviction.

items.title: 'Genuinely speechless': Appeal board's stunning claim in homophobia case

children.content: The appeal board in the Lance Collard homophobia case says homophobic and racist language is commonplace in the AFL due to the "highly competitive" nature of the game.

image.altText: Lance Collard stands with both arms in the air during a St Kilda vs West Coast Eagles AFL match in 2025.

items.title: How Aussies near you are coping with the fuel crisis

children.content: Petrol prices are reshaping Australian life in ways big and small — from a grandmother's isolation to a farmer's future.

items.title: Tobacco ban in the UK could create smoke-free generation

children.content: Anyone born after 2008 will be banned from buying cigarettes or vapes for life in the UK when a new bill becomes law.

items.title: Farmers say an easy solution to the mouse plague has been rejected

are being rejected by the national chemical regulator.

items.title: Geelong refinery boss defends soaring fuel profit margins saying 'they need to make money'

children.content: The Viva Energy refinery provides about 10 per cent of Australia's fuel. Its chief executive, Scott Wyatt, chats to Alan Kohler about the company's future in the wake of the Geelong fire.

items.title: The legacy of once-banned soldiers that hides in plain sight

children.content: Thousands of Indian soldiers served alongside Anzac forces at Gallipoli, leaving their legacy in the "puggaree" — a traditional Indian headwrap that was adapted to adorn the slouch hats worn by the Australian army.

image.altText: Six men with red and white striped turbans in khaki uniforms with red and white around their waist and long white socks.
